schema.appDescription: Calculate credit card EMI interest cost and compare with personal loan. Understand the true cost of converting credit card purchases to EMIs.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Credit Card EMI Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see how numbers evolve year by year.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for you.

faq.q: What is the interest rate on credit card EMIs in India?
faq.a: Credit card EMI interest rates range from 12% to 36% p.a. depending on the bank and offer. 'No-cost EMI' schemes (available on electronics, appliances) waive interest but merchants often inflate the base price. Banks also charge processing fees (₹99–₹299) for EMI conversion.

faq.q: Is credit card EMI better than personal loan?
faq.a: Personal loan rates (10–18% p.a.) are typically lower than credit card EMI rates (18–36%). For purchases above ₹50,000 over 12+ months, a personal loan is usually cheaper. Credit card EMI is convenient but costlier — the ease comes at a price.

faq.q: What happens if I miss a credit card EMI?
faq.a: Missing a CC EMI reverts the entire outstanding balance to the card's full interest rate (typically 36–42% p.a.) and attracts late payment charges (₹500–₹1,300). Your CIBIL score drops significantly. Always keep auto-debit for minimum payment to avoid this.

faq.q: Is the Credit Card EMI Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, completely free with no registration on CalcPhi.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We never store your financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Credit Card EMI Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: ₹50,000 laptop on 12-month credit card EMI at 24%
examples.text: ₹50,000 at 24% for 12 months: EMI = ₹4,741. Total interest = ₹6,892. Total payment = ₹56,892. Effective rate is 24% p.a. Compare with HDFC personal loan at 12%: EMI = ₹4,440, total interest = ₹3,280 — saving ₹3,600.

examples.heading: No-cost EMI — is it really free?
examples.text: Flipkart/Amazon 'no-cost EMI' on ₹30,000 TV for 6 months: you pay ₹5,000/month with no stated interest. However, the product price on no-cost EMI is often ₹1,500–₹2,500 higher than cash price. Effective cost: 10–16% p.a. Not free — just structured differently.
